name: Carcassonne
shortDescription: Europe's largest and best-preserved medieval walled city, with 52 towers.
description: The fortified city of Carcassonne has origins in the 1st century BC but its spectacular double ring of walls and 52 towers dates mainly from the 12th–13th centuries under the Trencavel viscounts and the French crown. Restored by Viollet-le-Duc in the 19th century, it presents one of the most complete pictures of a medieval fortified town in Europe. The Château Comtal within the walls adds a castle-within-a-city dimension.

name: Walls of Ávila
shortDescription: Spain's most complete medieval city walls, with 88 semicircular towers.
description: Encircling the entire historic city, the walls of Ávila are the best-preserved complete medieval city walls in Spain and among the finest in the world. Built under Alfonso VI of Castile around 1090, they stretch 2.5 km, rise up to 12 metres, and feature 88 semicircular towers and nine gateways. The walls uniquely incorporate the Cathedral's apse as an eastern defensive tower.

name: Old Town of Dubrovnik
shortDescription: The 'Pearl of the Adriatic' — a perfect Renaissance walled city on the Dalmatian coast.
description: The Republic of Ragusa maintained its independence for centuries behind walls that remain almost perfectly intact. The limestone circuit — up to 6 metres thick in places — encompasses towers, bastions, and the Minceta, Fort Lawrence, and Revelin fortresses. The wealth of Ragusa funded exquisite Renaissance architecture within, making Dubrovnik's old town one of the finest ensembles in Europe.

name: Walls of Lucca
shortDescription: A complete Renaissance rampart circuit so wide it's now a tree-lined promenade.
description: Lucca's Renaissance walls, begun in 1504 and completed in 1645, are among the best-preserved in Italy and unique in that they were never breached in battle. So effective were they — aided by a wide outer earthwork — that they were never needed in earnest. Today the tree-lined 4.2 km circuit atop the walls serves as Lucca's favourite walkway and cycling path, with magnificent views of the city.

name: Medieval City of Rhodes
shortDescription: The Knights Hospitaller's magnificent Aegean fortress city.
description: The Knights Hospitaller occupied Rhodes from 1309 and transformed it into one of the Mediterranean's most powerful fortresses, withstanding two Ottoman sieges before surrendering in 1523. The Street of the Knights, Palace of the Grand Masters, and 4 km of walls represent an outstanding ensemble of Gothic, Byzantine, and Ottoman architecture. Suleiman the Magnificent finally captured the city with overwhelming force after a six-month siege.

name: Rothenburg ob der Tauber
shortDescription: Germany's best-preserved medieval walled town, seemingly frozen in the 15th century.
description: Rothenburg ob der Tauber is Germany's finest example of a preserved medieval town. Its 2.4 km of intact walls with walkable ramparts, 42 towers, and six original gates date primarily to the 14th–15th centuries. The town escaped industrialisation due to economic stagnation, inadvertently preserving its medieval character. After WWII damage was repaired with international help due to the town's architectural importance.

name: Ksar of Aït Benhaddou
shortDescription: A magnificent fortified Berber village of earthen clay towers on a hillside.
description: Aït Benhaddou is an outstanding example of southern Moroccan architecture — a fortified village (ksar) of earthen clay towers set on a hillside above the Ounila River along the old caravan route between the Sahara and Marrakesh. Its towers, decorated with geometric patterns, are built entirely from pise (rammed earth) and collectively form one of the most photogenic fortified settlements on earth.

name: Ark of Bukhara
shortDescription: The ancient citadel and royal city at the heart of Bukhara, Silk Road capital.
description: The Ark of Bukhara is a massive fortified palace-city that served as the seat of Bukharan rulers from the early centuries CE until the city's conquest by the Red Army in 1920. Within its thick earthen and brick walls stood palaces, mosques, stables, and a prison. The surrounding city of Bukhara was one of the most important cities on the ancient Silk Road, and its old town preserves an extraordinary collection of Islamic architecture.

name: Old Town of Tallinn
shortDescription: The best-preserved medieval walled city in northern Europe.
description: Tallinn's medieval fortifications — built under Danish, German, and later Swedish control — survive in remarkable completeness, with 1.9 km of walls, 26 towers, and several gates still standing. The town divides into the Toompea (cathedral hill) and the Lower Town, each with separate fortifications. Towers like Fat Margaret and Tall Hermann give the skyline its distinctive character.

name: Intramuros
shortDescription: The original walled city of Manila, built by Spanish colonisers on the shores of Manila Bay.
description: Intramuros — literally 'within the walls' — was the seat of Spanish colonial government in the Philippines for over 300 years. Its massive basalt stone walls, bastions, and moat were designed by Spanish military engineers. Fort Santiago at its northwestern corner controlled access from Manila Bay. The walls were severely damaged in WWII but have been partially restored and remain the historic core of Manila.
